evidence.q1: Sections are numbered 1 through 15 sequentially; no internal cross‑references fail to resolve.
evidence.q2: “Personal Data,” “Processing,” and “Personal Data Breach” are defined once in Section 2 and used consistently throughout.
evidence.q3: All time/quantity phrases are specific: “within 2 business days,” “within 24 hours,” “every 24 hours,” “within 30 days,” “at least 30 days’ notice,” “up to 30 days,” “365 days.”
evidence.q4: No archaic diction; prose is modern, e.g., “Vendor will maintain administrative, technical, and physical safeguards …”
evidence.q5: No sentence is internally contradictory or grammatically broken; all clauses parse cleanly.
evidence.q6: Phrasing register is uniform—active voice, plain English, and consistent use of “Vendor will”/“Vendor may” across all sections.
